layer.description: Layer-shell layer the bar is placed on.

layout.description:
Per-monitor bar layouts. Each entry targets a monitor by connector name
(e.g., `"DP-1"`) or `"*"` for all monitors. See [`BarLayout`] for the
full shape, including layout inheritance via `extends`.

## Example

```toml
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "*"
left = ["dashboard"]
center = ["clock"]
right = ["battery", "network", "volume", "systray"]

[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "HDMI-1"
extends = "*"
right = ["volume", "systray"]
```

module-gap.description: Gap between modules and groups on the bar.

padding.description:
Internal spacing along bar thickness.

- **Orientation**: Top/bottom (horizontal bar), left/right (vertical bar)

padding-ends.description:
Internal spacing at bar ends.

- **Orientation**: Left/right (horizontal bar), top/bottom (vertical bar)

rounding.description: Corner rounding level for the bar.

scale.description: Bar-specific scale multiplier for spacing, radius, and other bar elements.

BarGroup.description: Named group of modules. The name becomes a CSS ID selector.

name.description: Unique name for CSS targeting (becomes `#name` selector).

anyOf.description: A single module (plain or with custom CSS class).
A named group of modules with shared visual container.

BarItem.description:
One entry in a bar layout section (`left`, `center`, or `right`).

Three shapes are accepted, all interchangeable in the same array:

- A plain module name: `"clock"`
- A module with a CSS class for per-instance styling: `{ module = "clock", class = "primary" }`
- A named group that wraps several modules in a shared container, addressable by CSS ID

## Examples

```toml
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "*"

# Plain module
left = ["dashboard"]

# Mix of plain and classed modules on the same side
center = ["clock", { module = "clock", class = "secondary" }]

# Named group (renders inside a GTK container with CSS ID `#status`)
right = [{ name = "status", modules = ["battery", "network", "volume"] }]

# Groups can hold classed modules too
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "DP-2"
left = [{ name = "clocks", modules = [
  { module = "clock", class = "local" },
  { module = "world-clock", class = "remote" }
]}]
```

BarLayout.description:
Layout configuration for a bar on a specific monitor.

## Examples

```toml
# Single modules
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "*"
left = ["dashboard"]
center = ["clock"]
right = ["systray"]

# Module with custom CSS class for per-instance styling
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "DP-1"
left = [{ module = "clock", class = "primary-clock" }, "clock"]
center = ["media"]

# Grouped modules (share a visual container, CSS-targetable by name)
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "DP-2"
left = [{ name = "status", modules = ["battery", "network"] }]

# Groups can also contain classed modules
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "DP-3"
left = [{ name = "clocks", modules = [
  { module = "clock", class = "local" },
  { module = "world-clock", class = "remote" }
]}]

# Inherit from another layout
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "*"
left = ["dashboard"]
center = ["clock"]
right = ["systray"]

[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "HDMI-1"
extends = "*"
right = ["volume", "systray"]  # Override just this section

# Hide bar on a specific monitor
[[bar.layout]]
monitor = "HDMI-2"
show = false
```

extends.description: Inherit from another layout by its monitor value (e.g., `"*"`).

monitor.description: Monitor connector name (e.g., `"DP-1"`) or `"*"` for all monitors.

show.description: Whether the bar is visible on this monitor.

BarModule.description: Bar module name. Built-in modules or custom modules with a `custom-<id>` pattern.

BatteryConfig.description:
Battery level, charging state, and a dropdown with power-profile controls.

::: warning

This module uses `upower` (D-Bus) for battery information. Ensure `upower` daemon is running and exposes a battery device (verify with `upower --battery` or `upower --dump`).

:::

alert-icon.description: Icon shown when battery is not present or in an error state.

charging-icon.description: Icon shown when battery is charging.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ percent }}` - Battery level (0-100)

## Examples

- `"{{ percent }}%"` - "45%"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

level-icons.description:
Icons for battery levels from empty to full.

The percentage is divided evenly among icons. With 5 icons:
0-20% uses icons\[0\], 21-40% uses icons\[1\], etc.

thresholds.description:
Dynamic color thresholds based on battery percentage.

Entries are checked in order; the last matching entry wins for each
color slot. Use `below` for low-value warnings (e.g., low battery).

## Example

```toml
[[modules.battery.thresholds]]
below = 40
icon-color = "status-warning"

[[modules.battery.thresholds]]
below = 20
icon-color = "status-error"
label-color = "status-error"
```

disconnected-icon.description: Icon when Bluetooth is on but no devices connected.

enable-external.description:
Discover and control external monitors over DDC/CI (I²C).

External monitors appear as extra sliders in the brightness panel.
Requires the `i2c-dev` kernel module and read/write access to

the slow DDC scan if you only have an internal panel.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

left-click.description: Action on left click. Default opens the brightness dropdown.

level-icons.description:
Icons for brightness levels from low to maximum.

The percentage is divided evenly among icons. With 3 icons:
0-33% uses icons\[0\], 34-66% uses icons\[1\], 67-100% uses icons\[2\].

min-brightness.description:
Lower bound (percent) for the native brightness-adjust action.

Scrolling or clicking down never drops below this, so a dimmer cannot
reach a fully dark screen by accident. Use the brightness-toggle action
to intentionally blackout to 0%.

scroll-down.description:
Action on scroll down. Default lowers brightness by 5%, floored at
`min-brightness`.

scroll-up.description: Action on scroll up. Default raises brightness by 5%.

thresholds.description:
Dynamic color thresholds based on brightness percentage.

Entries are checked in order; the last matching entry wins for each
color slot. Use `below` for low-brightness warnings.

## Example

```toml

below = 20
icon-color = "status-warning"
label-color = "status-warning"
```

format.description:
Format string using strftime syntax.

## Common Specifiers

- `%H` - Hour (00-23)
- `%I` - Hour (01-12)
- `%M` - Minute (00-59)
- `%S` - Second (00-59)
- `%p` - AM/PM
- `%a` - Abbreviated weekday (Mon, Tue)
- `%A` - Full weekday (Monday)
- `%b` - Abbreviated month (Jan, Feb)
- `%B` - Full month (January)
- `%d` - Day of month (01-31)
- `%Y` - Year (2024)

## Examples

- `"%H:%M"` - "14:30"
- `"%I:%M %p"` - "02:30 PM"
- `"%a %b %d %I:%M %p"` - "Mon Jan 15 02:30 PM"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

oneOf.description: `--bg-surface-elevated` - Subtle elevation from surface (buttons on surface).

ColorValue.description: CSS token, hex color (#rgb, #rgba, #rrggbb, or #rrggbbaa), 'transparent', or 'auto'

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ percent }}` - CPU usage (0-100)
- `{{ freq_ghz }}` - Frequency of the busiest core (highest usage)
- `{{ avg_freq_ghz }}` - Average frequency across cores
- `{{ max_freq_ghz }}` - Maximum frequency among cores
- `{{ temp_c }}` - Temperature in Celsius (if available)
- `{{ temp_f }}` - Temperature in Fahrenheit (if available)

## Examples

- `"{{ percent }}%"` - "45%"
- `"{{ percent }}% @ {{ freq_ghz }}GHz"` - "45% @ 3.2GHz"
- `"{{ percent }}% {{ temp_c }}C"` - "45% 62C"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation. Set to 0 to disable.

poll-interval-ms.description:
Polling interval in milliseconds.

Faster polling increases CPU usage.

temp-sensor.description:
Temperature sensor label.

Use `"auto"` for automatic detection, or specify a
label (e.g., `"Tctl"`, `"Package id 0"`).

Run `sensors` to see available labels.

thresholds.description:
Dynamic color thresholds based on CPU usage percentage.

Entries are checked in order; the last matching entry wins for each
color slot. Use `above` for high-value warnings (e.g., high CPU usage).

## Example

```toml
[[modules.cpu.thresholds]]
above = 70
icon-color = "status-warning"
label-color = "status-warning"

[[modules.cpu.thresholds]]
above = 90
icon-color = "status-error"
label-color = "status-error"
```

CustomModuleDefinition.description:
User-defined module that runs a shell command and renders the output in the bar.

Full walkthrough with examples in `docs/guide/custom-modules.md`.

class-format.description:
Format string for dynamic CSS classes.

Supports the same Jinja2 syntax as `format`. The formatted result is
split on whitespace and each word is added as a CSS class.

Combined with the `class` field from JSON output (if present).

## Example

```toml
class-format = "volume-{{ alt }}"
# Output: {"alt": "muted"} → adds class "volume-muted"
```

color-map.description:
Map of per-state color overrides keyed by the `alt` field value.

Mirrors `icon-map`: the `alt` value from JSON output is looked up in
this map and the colors set for that state override the module's
static colors, letting a single widget cycle its colors alongside its
icon as its state changes. Use `"default"` as a fallback key. Any
color left unset for the matched state falls back to the module's
static color of the same name.

## Example

```toml
icon-map = { muted = "audio-volume-muted-symbolic" }
color-map = { muted = { icon-color = "red" }, default = { icon-color = "green" } }

# Output: {"percentage": 50, "alt": "muted"}
# Result: muted icon rendered in red
```

command.description:
Shell command to execute.

The command runs via `sh -c` and should output to stdout.
Stderr is discarded. Commands have a 30-second timeout.

## Output Parsing

- If output starts with `{` or `[`: parsed as JSON
- Otherwise: treated as plain text

## Behavior by Mode

- **poll**: Executed every `interval-ms` milliseconds
- **watch**: Spawned once, each stdout line triggers a display update.
  Restarts are controlled by `restart-policy`.

format.description:
Format string for the label using Jinja2 template syntax.

## Variables

- `{{ output }}` - Raw command output
- `{{ field }}` - JSON field access
- `{{ nested.field }}` - Nested field access
- `{{ items.0 }}` - Array index access

## Filters

- `{{ val | default('fallback') }}` - Fallback for missing values
- `{{ "%02d" | format(val) }}` - Zero-padding
- `{{ val | upper }}`, `| lower`, `| trim` - String transforms

## Examples

- `"{{ output }}°C"` - Plain text: "72°C"
- `"{{ percentage }}%"` - JSON field: "75%"
- `"{{ data.temp }}°C"` - Nested: "22°C"

If JSON output contains a `text` field, it overrides this format.

hide-if-empty.description:
Hide module when output is empty, "0", or "false".

When enabled, the module (including its gap in the bar layout) is
completely hidden if the output indicates an empty/disabled state.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color.

icon-map.description:
Map of icon names keyed by the `alt` field value.

Requires JSON output with an `alt` field. The `alt` value is looked up
in this map. Use `"default"` as a fallback key.

**Priority**: `icon-map[alt]` takes precedence over `icon-names[percentage]`,
allowing state-specific icons to override percentage-based icons.

## Example

```toml
# Volume with muted state override
icon-names = ["vol-0", "vol-33", "vol-66", "vol-100"]
icon-map = { "muted" = "audio-volume-muted-symbolic" }

# Output: {"percentage": 50, "alt": "muted"}
# Result: Uses "audio-volume-muted-symbolic" (alt match beats percentage)

# Output: {"percentage": 50}
# Result: Uses "vol-33" (percentage-based, no alt)
```

icon-name.description:
Static symbolic icon name.

Used when `icon-names` and `icon-map` don't provide a match.
Should be a symbolic icon name from the icon theme (e.g., `"ld-gpu-symbolic"`).

## Example

```toml
icon-name = "ld-temperature-symbolic"
```

icon-names.description:
Array of icon names indexed by percentage (0-100).

Requires JSON output with a `percentage` field (0-100).
The array is divided evenly across the percentage range.

## Resolution

For N icons, icon at index `floor(percentage * N / 101)` is selected:

- 4 icons: 0-24% → [0], 25-49% → [1], 50-74% → [2], 75-100% → [3]
- 5 icons: 0-19% → [0], 20-39% → [1], 40-59% → [2], 60-79% → [3], 80-100% → [4]

## Example

```toml
icon-names = [
  "battery-empty-symbolic",
  "battery-caution-symbolic",
  "battery-low-symbolic",
  "battery-good-symbolic",
  "battery-full-symbolic"
]
```

id.description:
Unique identifier for this module.

Referenced in bar layouts as `custom-<id>`. Must be unique across
all custom module definitions.

## Example

```toml
[[modules.custom]]
id = "gpu-temp"

# Reference in layout:
# layout = ["custom-gpu-temp", "clock"]
```

interval-ms.description:
Polling interval in milliseconds.

Only applies to `poll` mode. Ignored in `watch` mode.

Set to `0` for manual polling mode: no timer is started. In manual
mode, the command still runs once at startup.

label-max-length.description:
Maximum label length in characters before truncation.

When exceeded, label is truncated with ellipsis. Set to `0` to disable.

left-click.description:
Action performed on left click.

Supports shell commands and dropdown toggles:
- `"pavucontrol"` — runs a shell command
- `"dropdown:audio"` — toggles the named dropdown panel
- `""` — no action (default)

If `on-action` is set, it runs after shell commands complete.

middle-click.description:
Action performed on middle click.

Supports shell commands and dropdown toggles (see `left-click`).
If `on-action` is set, it runs after shell commands complete.

mode.description:
Execution mode for the command.

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `poll` | Run command every `interval-ms` (default) |
| `watch` | Spawn long-running process, update on each stdout line |

Use `poll` for commands that return current state and exit.
Use `watch` for commands that stream updates (e.g., `pactl subscribe`).

on-action.description:
Shell command to run after any click/scroll action completes.

Executes after the action handler finishes, and its output updates
the display immediately. Useful for reflecting state changes without
waiting for the next poll interval.

## Example

```toml
# Volume control with immediate feedback
scroll-up = "pactl set-sink-volume @DEFAULT_SINK@ +5%"
scroll-down = "pactl set-sink-volume @DEFAULT_SINK@ -5%"
on-action = '''
vol=$(pactl get-sink-volume @DEFAULT_SINK@ | grep -oP '\d+(?=%)' | head -1)
echo "{\"percentage\": $vol}"
'''
```

restart-interval-ms.description:
Base restart delay in milliseconds for watch mode.

Only applies to `watch` mode. Ignored in `poll` mode.

Used when `restart-policy` is `on-exit` or `on-failure`.
Delay increases exponentially on rapid failures, capped at 30 seconds.

restart-policy.description:
Restart policy for watch mode.

Only applies to `watch` mode. Ignored in `poll` mode.

| Policy | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `never` | Do not restart after exit |
| `on-exit` | Restart after any exit |
| `on-failure` | Restart only after non-zero/signal exit |

right-click.description:
Action performed on right click.

Supports shell commands and dropdown toggles (see `left-click`).
If `on-action` is set, it runs after shell commands complete.

scroll-down.description:
Action performed on scroll down.

Supports shell commands and dropdown toggles (see `left-click`).
Scroll events are debounced (50ms) to coalesce rapid scrolls.
If `on-action` is set, it runs after shell commands complete.

scroll-up.description:
Action performed on scroll up.

Supports shell commands and dropdown toggles (see `left-click`).
Scroll events are debounced (50ms) to coalesce rapid scrolls.
If `on-action` is set, it runs after shell commands complete.

tooltip-format.description:
Format string for the tooltip (hover text).

Supports the same Jinja2 syntax as `format`. If not set, no tooltip is shown.
If JSON output contains a `tooltip` field, it overrides this format.

## Example

```toml
format = "{{ percentage }}%"
tooltip-format = "Volume: {{ percentage }}% on {{ device }}"
```

DashboardConfig.description: Quick-access button with a distro icon; opens the dashboard dropdown.

battery-critical.description: Battery percent at or below which the dashboard battery shows critical.

battery-warning.description: Battery percent at or below which the dashboard battery shows warning.

dropdown-lock-command.description: Shell command for the lock button in the dashboard dropdown.

dropdown-logout-command.description: Shell command for the logout button in the dashboard dropdown.

dropdown-poweroff-command.description: Shell command for the power-off button in the dashboard dropdown.

dropdown-reboot-command.description: Shell command for the reboot button in the dashboard dropdown.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

temp-error.description: CPU temperature (°C) at which the dashboard temp ring turns error.

temp-warning.description: CPU temperature (°C) at which the dashboard temp ring turns warning.

usage-error.description: CPU/RAM/disk usage percent at which the dashboard rings turn error.

usage-warning.description: CPU/RAM/disk usage percent at which the dashboard rings turn warning.

DisplayMode.description: What identifies a workspace in the UI.

oneOf.description: Show icon from `workspace-map` (falls back to label if unmapped).
Show nothing - only app icons visible.

DropdownSize.description:
Size override for a dropdown foldout panel.

Each field is optional: when unset the dropdown keeps its built-in default
size (scaled by the global scale). A [`Size`] may be a scale multiplier of
the built-in base (e.g. `1.5`) or an absolute pixel length (e.g. `"480px"`).

height.description:
Panel height override. Unset uses the built-in default height. Has no
effect on dropdowns whose height grows to fit their content.

width.description: Panel width override. Unset uses the built-in default width.

DropdownsConfig.description:
Per-dropdown foldout panel sizing.

Each field overrides the size of one bar widget dropdown. Unset fields keep
the built-in default (scaled by the global scale).

brightness.description: Brightness dropdown panel size. Height grows to fit content.

calendar.description: Calendar dropdown panel size. Height grows to fit content.

dashboard.description: Dashboard dropdown panel size. Height grows to fit content.

mail.description: Mail dropdown panel size. Height grows to fit content.

ExecutionMode.description: Execution mode for custom module commands.

oneOf.description:
Run command at regular intervals defined by `interval-ms`.

Best for commands that complete quickly and return current state
(e.g., reading a file, querying system status).

Spawn long-running process and update display on each stdout line.

Best for event-driven updates without polling overhead
(e.g., `pactl subscribe`, `inotifywait`, `tail -f`).
Configure `restart-policy` to control restarts after exit.
Scale to cover entire display, cropping excess.
Scale to fit within display, letterboxing if needed.
Stretch to exactly fill, ignoring aspect ratio.
Scale to cover entire display, cropping excess.
Scale to fit within display, letterboxing if needed.
Stretch to exactly fill, ignoring aspect ratio.

FontWeightClass.description:
Font weight class for typography.

Maps to CSS classes like `.weight-normal`, `.weight-bold`, etc.
Uses the existing `--weight-*` tokens defined in SCSS.

FrequencyHz.description:
Frequency value in Hz, minimum 1 Hz.

Cross-field constraints (high_cutoff > low_cutoff, samplerate/2 > high_cutoff)
are validated at the service builder.

GeneralConfig.description: Shell-wide settings that don't belong to any specific module.

font-mono.description: Monospace font family for code and technical content.

font-sans.description: Sans-serif font family for UI text and labels.

tearing-mode.description:
Demote overlay surfaces to allow compositor screen tearing.

When enabled, surfaces that would normally use the `overlay` layer
are demoted to `top`, allowing fullscreen games to use direct scanout.

active-color.description:
Color for the active (focused) workspace.

Applied to icons and labels. In `background` indicator mode,
also used as the button background.

active-indicator.description: Visual indicator for the active workspace.

active-on-other-monitor-color.description:
Active on other minitor indicator color.

Only applies when `highlight-active-on-other-monitor` is `true`.

app-icon-map.description:
Application icon mapping with glob pattern support.

Maps window class or title to symbolic icon names. Supports:
- No prefix: Matches window class (e.g., `"*firefox*"`)
- `class:` prefix: Explicit class match (e.g., `"class:org.mozilla.*"`)
- `title:` prefix: Matches window title (e.g., `"title:*YouTube*"`)

User mappings are merged with built-in defaults for common applications.

## Example

```toml
[modules.hyprland-workspaces.app-icon-map]
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"title:*YouTube*" = "ld-youtube-symbolic"
```

app-icons-dedupe.description:
Deduplicate application icons within a workspace.

When true, shows only one icon per unique window class.
When false, shows an icon for every window.

app-icons-empty.description:
Icon shown for empty workspaces when `app-icons-show` is enabled.

When a workspace has no windows but is displayed (via `min-workspace-count`),
this icon appears as a placeholder.

app-icons-fallback.description: Fallback icon for applications not matched by `app-icon-map`.

app-icons-show.description:
Show application icons for windows in each workspace.

When enabled, displays icons for running applications.
Icons are resolved via `app-icon-map` configuration.

border-color.description: Border color for the workspaces container.

border-show.description: Display border around the workspaces container.

container-bg-color.description: Background color for the workspaces container.

display-mode.description:
What identifies each workspace button.

- `label`: Shows workspace number (or name if `label-use-name` is true)
- `icon`: Shows icon from `workspace-map` (falls back to label if unmapped)
- `none`: Shows nothing - only app icons visible

divider.description:
Text separator between workspace identity and app icons.

Only shown when both `display-mode` is not `none` and `app-icons-show`
is enabled. Common values: `"|"`, `"·"`, `"-"`.

empty-color.description:
Color for empty workspaces.

Applied to the empty placeholder icon and labels.

highlight-active-on-other-monitor.description:
Highlight workspaces active on other monitors with a different color.

When true, workspaces active on a different monior are highlicted differently.
When false, workspaces active on a another monitor are not specially highlighted.

This setting only makes sense when `monitor-specific` is false.

icon-gap.description:
Gap between app icons within a workspace button.

Only applies to spacing between app icons.

icon-size.description:
Workspace icon size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

Applies to workspace identity icons and custom icons from `workspace-map`.

label-size.description:
Workspace label and divider size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

Applies to workspace number/name labels and the divider text.

label-use-name.description:
Use workspace name instead of number when displaying labels.

Only applies when `display-mode = "label"` or as fallback for unmapped
workspaces in `display-mode = "icon"`.

left-click.description: Action on left-clicking a workspace. Default focuses it.

min-workspace-count.description:
Minimum number of workspace buttons to display.

When set to 0 (default), only active and occupied workspaces are shown.
When set to N, at least N buttons are always visible, with empty ones
using `empty-color` styling.

monitor-specific.description:
Show only workspaces belonging to the bar's monitor.

When true, each bar shows only its monitor's workspaces.
When false, all workspaces from all monitors are shown.

numbering.description:
How workspace numbers are displayed.

- `absolute`: Show actual Hyprland workspace IDs (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6...)
- `relative`: Show numbers relative to monitor's starting workspace.
  If a monitor has workspaces 4, 5, 6 assigned, they display as 1, 2, 3.
  Useful when keybinds use per-monitor numbering.

occupied-color.description:
Color for occupied workspaces (has windows but not focused).

Applied to icons and labels.

scroll-down.description: Action on scrolling down over the module. Default focuses the next workspace.

scroll-up.description: Action on scrolling up over the module. Default focuses the previous workspace.

show-special.description:
Include special workspaces (scratchpads) in the display.

Special workspaces have negative IDs in Hyprland.

urgent-mode.description:
Where the urgent pulse is applied.

- `workspace`: Entire workspace pulses (default)
- `application`: Only the app icon(s) belonging to the urgent window
  pulse, falling back to `workspace` when app icons are disabled

urgent-show.description:
Pulse animation on workspaces with urgent windows.

When a window requests attention (e.g., terminal bell), the workspace
button pulses until you switch to it.

workspace-ignore.description:
Workspaces to hide from the display.

Glob patterns matching workspace IDs. Examples:
- `"10"` - hide workspace 10
- `"1?"` - hide workspaces 10-19

workspace-map.description:
Per-workspace icon and color overrides.

Keys are workspace IDs (use negative for special workspaces).

## Example

```toml
[modules.hyprland-workspaces.workspace-map]
1 = { icon = "ld-globe-symbolic", color = "#4a90d9" }
2 = { icon = "ld-terminal-symbolic" }
```

workspace-padding.description:
Padding for workspace content along the bar direction. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"8px"`).

For horizontal bars, controls horizontal (left/right) padding.
For vertical bars, controls vertical (top/bottom) padding.

auto-schedule.description:
Automatically enable the filter at night and disable it during the day,
based on local sunrise/sunset computed from `latitude`/`longitude`.

While enabled, the module drives the filter on the solar schedule. A
manual click toggles an override that lasts until the next sunrise or
sunset, after which the schedule resumes.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ status }}` - Filter status text (On, Off)
- `{{ temp }}` - Current temperature in Kelvin (shows "--" when disabled)
- `{{ gamma }}` - Current gamma percentage (shows "--" when disabled)
- `{{ config_temp }}` - Configured temperature (always available)
- `{{ config_gamma }}` - Configured gamma (always available)

## Examples

- `"{{ status }}"` - "On"
- `"{{ temp }}K {{ gamma }}%"` - "4500K 80%"
- `"{{ status }} ({{ temp }}K)"` - "On (4500K)"

gamma.description: Display gamma percentage when filter is enabled. Range: 0-200.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

icon-off.description: Icon when filter is disabled (showing normal daylight colors).

icon-on.description: Icon when filter is enabled (showing warm night colors).

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

latitude.description:
Latitude for the sunrise/sunset schedule, in decimal degrees
(north positive). Range: -90 to 90. Only used when `auto-schedule` is on.

left-click.description: Action on left click. Default toggles blue light filter.

longitude.description:
Longitude for the sunrise/sunset schedule, in decimal degrees
(east positive). Range: -180 to 180. Only used when `auto-schedule` is on.

temperature.description: Color temperature in Kelvin when filter is enabled. Range: 1000-20000.

oneOf.description: Icon before label (left for horizontal, top for vertical bars).
Icon after label (right for horizontal, bottom for vertical bars).
Use per-notification images when provided, otherwise Wayle's mapped icon.
Always use Wayle's mapped icons regardless of what the app provides.
Use the full application icon chain, falling back to mapped if unavailable.

IdleInhibitConfig.description:
Toggle that prevents screen dim, lock, and suspend while active.

Controllable from the CLI: `wayle idle on|off|duration|remaining|status`.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ state }}` - Inhibitor state text (On, Off)
- `{{ remaining }}` - Time remaining (e.g., "45m", shows "--" when indefinite)
- `{{ duration }}` - Total duration (e.g., "60m", shows "--" when indefinite)

## Examples

- `"{{ state }}"` - "On"
- `"{{ remaining }}/{{ duration }}"` - "45m/60m"
- `"{{ state }} ({{ remaining }})"` - "On (45m)"

icon-active.description: Icon when idle inhibitor is active.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

icon-inactive.description: Icon when idle inhibitor is inactive.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

left-click.description: Action on left click. Default toggles indefinite idle inhibit.

right-click.description: Action on right click. Default toggles timed idle inhibit.

startup-duration.description: Duration in minutes when service starts. 0 means indefinite.

KeybindModeConfig.description: Current keybind-mode indicator for modal compositors.

auto-hide.description: Automatically hide module when no mode is active.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ mode }}` - Current keybind mode name (shows "default" when inactive)

## Examples

- `"{{ mode }}"` - "resize"
- `"Mode: {{ mode }}"` - "Mode: resize"
- `"[{{ mode }}]"` - "[resize]"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ layout }}` - Raw layout name from the compositor (e.g., "English (US)")
- `{{ alias }}` - User-defined alias from `layout-alias-map`, falls back to `{{ layout }}`

## Examples

- `"{{ layout }}"` - "English (US)"
- `"{{ alias }}"` - "EN" (with alias map configured)

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

oneOf.description: Show the name when set, fall back to the index.
Show only the name; unnamed workspaces show nothing.
Show both, joined as `"1: web"`. Unnamed workspaces show the index alone.

Layer.description: Layer-shell layer a window is placed on, from furthest back to furthest front.

oneOf.description: Below everything else, used for wallpapers and ambient surfaces.
Above everything, including fullscreen application windows.

LockBackground.description: How the lock screen background is rendered.

LockConfig.description:
Lock screen: a secure session lock rendered by Wayle via `ext-session-lock-v1`.

When `enabled`, Wayle locks the session in response to the logind `Lock`
signal (`loginctl lock-session`), the `wayle lock` CLI, or the shell IPC
`lock()` method. The lock surface grabs all input and survives client
crashes (the compositor shows a solid color), unlike a layer-shell overlay.

background-mode.description: How the background is drawn: solid color, an image, or the wallpaper.

blank-timeout-ms.description:
Black out the lock screen after this idle time (milliseconds, `0` =
never). This is a visual blank that hides the clock/prompt and dismisses
on any key; true display power-off (DPMS) is left to your idle daemon.

blur.description: Gaussian blur radius applied to image/wallpaper backgrounds (0 = none).

enabled.description:
Let Wayle handle session locking. When off, lock requests are ignored
and an external locker (e.g. hyprlock) stays responsible.

grace-period-ms.description:
Grace window after locking during which the screen unlocks without a
password (milliseconds, `0` = always require the password).

max-attempts.description:
Maximum failed password attempts before further input is blocked
(`0` = unlimited). The screen stays locked regardless.

pam-service.description:
PAM service name used to authenticate the unlock. Distro-dependent:
`system-auth` (Arch/Fedora), `login`, or a custom `/etc/pam.d` entry.

show-clock.description: Show a clock on the lock screen.

show-failed-attempts.description: Show the failed-attempt count on the lock screen.

MailAccount.description:
One mail account in the `[modules.mail]` per-account breakdown.

Each account has its own notmuch query; the dropdown shows the per-account
unread counts and the bar shows their sum.

## Example

```toml
[[modules.mail.accounts]]
name = "Work"
query = "folder:work/INBOX and tag:unread"
provider = "gmail"
```

icon.description: Optional icon override. Empty uses the provider's default icon.

name.description: Display name shown in the dropdown.

provider.description: Provider, selecting the default brand icon.

query.description: notmuch query whose match count is this account's unread total.

MailConfig.description:
Unread mail count, backed by a notmuch query.

Runs `notmuch count <query>` and re-queries whenever the maildir changes
(event-driven via an inotify watch on the notmuch database path). Hidden
while the count is zero when `hide-when-zero` is set.

accounts.description:
Per-account unread breakdown shown in the mail dropdown. Each account
has its own notmuch query and a provider (for its icon). When set, the
bar count/label is the sum across accounts and `query` is ignored.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ count }}` - Number of messages matching the query

## Examples

- `"{{ count }}"` - "3"

hide-when-zero.description: Hide the module entirely while the count is zero.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

left-click.description:
Action on left click. Defaults to opening the per-account dropdown; set
to empty for no action, or a shell command (e.g. your mail client).

notify.description:
Fire a desktop notification (via `notify-send`) when the unread count
rises — i.e. new mail arrives. One notification per newly-arrived
message (capped per burst), showing its sender and subject. With
`accounts` configured, each notification uses that account's provider
icon; otherwise the module icon is used.

notify-body.description:
Notification body when new mail arrives. Same placeholders as
`notify-summary`.

notify-summary.description:
Notification summary when new mail arrives.

## Placeholders

- `{{ sender }}` - Message sender (name or address)
- `{{ subject }}` - Message subject
- `{{ count }}` - Total messages matching the query
- `{{ new }}` - How many arrived since the last count

query.description:
notmuch search query whose match count is shown.

Any query `notmuch count` accepts, e.g. `tag:unread`,
`tag:unread and tag:inbox`, `folder:work and tag:unread`.

Ignored when `accounts` is non-empty — the bar count is then the sum of
the per-account queries.

MailProvider.description: Mail provider, used to pick a default brand icon for an account.

oneOf.description: Generic mailbox; uses the plain mail glyph.

active-indicator.description: How the active tag is marked.

app-icon-map.description:
Window-to-icon mappings for the application icons.

Keys are glob patterns matched against a client's app id, or `title:`
patterns matched against its title. Values are symbolic icon names.

## Example

```toml
[modules.mango-workspaces.app-icon-map]
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"title:*YouTube*" = "si-youtube-symbolic"
```

app-icons-dedupe.description: Collapse clients that share an application to a single icon.

app-icons-empty.description: Icon shown when a tag has no clients.

app-icons-fallback.description: Icon for clients not matched by `app-icon-map`.

app-icons-show.description: Show an application icon per client on each tag.

border-color.description: Border color when the border is shown.

border-show.description: Draw a border around the tag container.

container-bg-color.description: Background color of the tag container.

display-mode.description: What identifies each tag: its label, an icon, or nothing.

divider.description: Text shown between the tag label and its application icons.

hide-empty.description: Hide tags that hold no clients and are not active.

icon-gap.description: Spacing between application icons. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"4px"`).

icon-size.description: Application icon size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

label-size.description: Tag label text size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

left-click.description: Action for a left click on a tag.

middle-click.description: Action for a middle click on a tag.

min-tag-count.description:
Always show tags up to this one-based index, even when empty.

`0` shows only occupied or active tags (subject to `hide-empty`). A
value above the compositor's tag count just shows every tag.

occupied-color.description: Color of tags that hold clients but are not active.

right-click.description: Action for a right click on a tag.

scroll-down.description: Action for scrolling down over the tag container.

scroll-up.description: Action for scrolling up over the tag container.

tag-map.description:
Per-tag icon and color overrides, keyed by one-based tag index.

## Example

```toml
[modules.mango-workspaces.tag-map.1]
label = "web"
icon = "ld-globe-symbolic"
color = "#4a90d9"

[modules.mango-workspaces.tag-map.2]
label = "term"
icon = "ld-terminal-symbolic"
```

tag-padding.description: Padding around each tag button, in rem.

urgent-mode.description: Whether urgency is tracked per tag or per application.

urgent-show.description: Highlight tags whose clients requested attention.

MediaConfig.description: Now-playing title and playback controls for the active MPRIS player.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ title }}` - Track title
- `{{ artist }}` - Artist name(s)
- `{{ album }}` - Album name
- `{{ status }}` - Playback status text (Playing, Paused, Stopped)
- `{{ status_icon }}` - Playback status icon character

## Examples

- `"{{ title }} - {{ artist }}"` - "Bohemian Rhapsody - Queen"
- `"{{ status_icon }} {{ title }}"` - "▶ Bohemian Rhapsody"
- `"{{ artist }}: {{ title }} ({{ album }})"` - "Queen: Bohemian Rhapsody (A Night at the Opera)"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

icon-name.description: Symbolic icon name for default mode.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

player-icons.description:
Custom player-to-icon mappings for application-mapped mode.

Keys are glob patterns matching MPRIS bus names, values are icon names
from the installed icon set. These override built-in mappings when
matched.

## Example

```toml
[modules.media.player-icons]
"*spotify*" = "si-spotify-symbolic"
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"*.mpv" = "ld-play-circle-symbolic"
```

player-priority.description:
Preferred player priority order as glob patterns matching bus names.

When no player is manually selected, this determines which player
becomes active. Patterns are checked in order; first match wins.
If no pattern matches, the first playing player is selected.

## Example

```toml
[modules.media]
player-priority = ["*spotify*", "*firefox*"]
```

players-ignored.description:
Player bus name patterns to exclude from discovery. Requires a restart
to take effect.

## Example

```toml
[modules.media]
players-ignored = ["*chromium*", "*discord*"]
```

MediaIconType.description: Icon display mode for the media module.

oneOf.description: Dynamic icon from media player's desktop entry, falling back to icon-name.
Spinning disc icon that animates during playback. Uses slightly more CPU.
Maps player to icon via glob patterns, with built-in mappings for common players.

MicrophoneConfig.description: Microphone input level and mute toggle.

icon-active.description: Icon shown when microphone is active (unmuted).

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

icon-muted.description: Icon shown when microphone is muted.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

middle-click.description: Action on middle click. Default toggles input mute.

thresholds.description:
Dynamic color thresholds based on microphone volume percentage.

Entries are checked in order; the last matching entry wins for each
color slot. Use `above` for high-value warnings (e.g., high input gain).

## Example

```toml
[[modules.microphone.thresholds]]
above = 70
icon-color = "status-warning"
label-color = "status-warning"

[[modules.microphone.thresholds]]
above = 90
icon-color = "status-error"
label-color = "status-error"
```

anyOf.description: Module with a custom CSS class.

ModuleRef.description:
Reference to a module, optionally with a custom CSS class.

## Examples

```toml
# Plain module (just the name)
left = ["clock"]

# Module with custom CSS class
left = [{ module = "clock", class = "primary-clock" }]
```

ModulesConfig.description: Configuration for all available Wayle modules.

custom.description:
Custom user-defined modules, each backed by a shell command. See
[`CustomModuleDefinition`] for all fields (id, command, interval, click
actions, icons, etc.). Reference them in a layout with `custom-<id>`.

## Example

```toml
[[modules.custom]]
id = "gpu-temp"
command = "nvidia-smi --query-gpu=temperature.gpu --format=csv,noheader"
interval-ms = 5000
icon-name = "ld-thermometer-symbolic"

[[modules.custom]]
id = "weather"
command = "curl -s wttr.in/?format=%t"
interval-ms = 600000
```

fit-mode.description: Image scaling mode for this monitor.

wallpaper.description: Wallpaper image path for this monitor.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Download Placeholders

- `{{ down_kib }}` - Download speed in KiB/s
- `{{ down_mib }}` - Download speed in MiB/s
- `{{ down_gib }}` - Download speed in GiB/s
- `{{ down_auto }}` - Download speed with auto unit (e.g., "1.5 MiB/s")

## Upload Placeholders

- `{{ up_kib }}` - Upload speed in KiB/s
- `{{ up_mib }}` - Upload speed in MiB/s
- `{{ up_gib }}` - Upload speed in GiB/s
- `{{ up_auto }}` - Upload speed with auto unit (e.g., "256 KiB/s")

## Other Placeholders

- `{{ interface }}` - Interface name (e.g., "wlan0")

## Examples

- `"{{ down_auto }} {{ up_auto }}"` - "1.5 MiB/s 256 KiB/s"
- `"D:{{ down_mib }} U:{{ up_mib }}"` - "D:1.5 U:0.2"
- `"{{ interface }}: {{ down_auto }}"` - "wlan0: 1.5 MiB/s"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color.

interface.description:
Network interface to monitor.

Use `"auto"` to select the first active interface, or specify an
interface name like `"eth0"` or `"wlan0"`.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation. Set to 0 to disable.

poll-interval-ms.description:
Polling interval in milliseconds.

Faster polling increases CPU usage.

NetworkConfig.description: Network connection status with a dropdown for switching connections.

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

label-show.description: Display connection label (SSID for WiFi, "Wired" for ethernet).

wifi-connected-icon.description: WiFi icon when connected but signal strength unavailable.

wifi-signal-icons.description:
WiFi signal strength icons from weak to excellent.

The signal percentage maps to icons: 0-25% uses icons\[0\], 26-50% uses
icons\[1\], etc.

active-color.description:
Color for the active (visible on its output) workspace.

In `background` indicator mode, also used as the button background.

active-indicator.description: Visual indicator for the active workspace.

app-icon-map.description:
Application icon mapping with glob pattern support.

Maps window `app_id` or title to symbolic icon names. Supports:
- No prefix: matches `app_id` (e.g. `"*firefox*"`)
- `app:` prefix: explicit `app_id` match (e.g. `"app:org.mozilla.*"`)
- `title:` prefix: matches window title (e.g. `"title:*YouTube*"`)

## Example

```toml
[modules.niri-workspaces.app-icon-map]
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"title:*YouTube*" = "ld-youtube-symbolic"
```

app-icons-dedupe.description:
Deduplicate application icons within a workspace.

When `true`, one icon per unique `app_id`. When `false`, one icon
per window.

app-icons-empty.description: Icon shown when a workspace has no application windows.

app-icons-fallback.description: Fallback icon for applications not matched by `app-icon-map`.

app-icons-show.description: Show application icons for windows on each workspace.

border-color.description: Border color for the workspaces container.

border-show.description: Display border around the workspaces container.

container-bg-color.description: Background color for the workspaces container.

display-mode.description:
What identifies each workspace button.

- `label` (default): show the workspace label per `label-strategy`
- `icon`: show an icon from `workspace-map` (falls back to label if unmapped)
- `none`: show nothing — only app icons visible (if enabled)

divider.description: Text separator between workspace identity and app icons.

empty-color.description: Color for empty workspaces and placeholder slots.

hide-trailing-empty.description:
Hide niri's auto-allocated trailing empty workspace.

Niri keeps one empty workspace at the tail of every output for
dynamic allocation.

icon-gap.description: Gap between app icons within a workspace button. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"4px"`).

icon-size.description:
Workspace icon size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

Applies to identity icons and custom icons from `workspace-map`.

label-size.description: Workspace label and divider size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

label-strategy.description:
How to compose the workspace label when `display-mode = "label"`.

- `index`: index only (`"1"`, `"2"`)
- `name-or-index` (default): name when set, index otherwise
- `name-only`: name only; unnamed workspaces show nothing
- `index-and-name`: `"1: web"` form; unnamed workspaces show the index alone

monitor-specific.description:
Show only workspaces on this bar's monitor.

When `true` (default), each bar shows only its own output's
workspaces. When `false`, all workspaces from every output are shown.

occupied-color.description: Color for occupied workspaces (have windows but not active).

urgent-mode.description:

- `application`: only the urgent app icon pulses, falling back to
  `workspace` when app icons are disabled

urgent-show.description: Pulse animation on workspaces with urgent windows.

workspace-ignore.description:

its index, then its stable id. Examples:

workspace-padding.description: Padding for workspace content along the bar direction. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"8px"`).

NotificationConfig.description: Notification center: icon in the bar, dropdown with history, DND toggle.

blocklist.description:
Glob patterns for app names whose notifications are blocked entirely.

enabled.description: Enable the notifications service and module.

icon-name.description: Icon shown when no notifications and DND is off.

icon-source.description:
How notification icons are resolved.

| Mode | Per-notification image | No image provided |
|------|----------------------|-------------------|
| `automatic` | Shows the image | Mapped icon |
| `mapped` | Ignored | Mapped icon |
| `application` | Shows the image | App's generic icon, then mapped fallback |

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

popup-close-behavior.description: What happens when the close button on a popup is clicked.

popup-duration.description:
Maximum popup display duration in milliseconds.

popup-gap.description:
Gap between stacked popups: a multiplier of the default 8px (`1.0` =
default) or absolute pixels.

popup-hover-pause.description: Pause popup auto-dismiss timer on hover.

popup-layer.description:
Layer-shell layer popup notifications are placed on.

to allow fullscreen tearing.

popup-margin-x.description:
Horizontal margin from screen edges. Accepts a scale multiplier or
pixels (e.g. `"12px"`).

popup-margin-y.description:
Vertical margin from screen edges. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels
(e.g. `"12px"`).

popup-max-visible.description: Maximum number of popups visible at once.

popup-monitor.description: Target monitor: "primary" or a connector name like "DP-1".

popup-shadow.description: Display drop shadow on popup cards.

popup-stacking-order.description: Order in which popups stack on screen.

popup-urgency-bar.description: Minimum urgency level that displays a colored urgency bar.

right-click.description: Action on right click. Default toggles Do Not Disturb.

thresholds.description:
Dynamic color thresholds based on notification count.

Entries are checked in order; the last matching entry wins for each
color slot. Use `above` for high-value warnings (e.g., many unread
notifications).

## Example

```toml
[[modules.notifications.thresholds]]
```

oneOf.description:

Useful when keybinds use per-monitor numbering (Shift+1 for ws 4, etc.).

presets.description: Reusable toast presets, each triggerable with `wayle toast --preset <id>`.

icon-power-saver.description: Icon shown while the power-saver profile is active.

- `{{ total_gib }}` - Total memory in GiB (e.g., "16.0")

RecorderConfig.description:
Native screen recorder backed by a GStreamer pipeline.

`wayle recorder start|stop|toggle|pause|status`.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

microphone.description: Capture the microphone in the recording.

Picker window height: a multiplier of the default 500px (`1.0` = default)

oneOf.description: Newest notifications appear closest to the configured position.

Storage mount targets accepted by `mount-point`.

Supports a single string for backwards compatibility or an array of paths.

StylingConfig.description: Theme, palette, and rounding tokens applied shell-wide. Changes recompile the stylesheet.

appearance.description:
Light/dark appearance mode. `Auto` follows each provider / the configured
palette; `Light`/`Dark` force the mode (static palettes swap to their
built-in light/dark variant when one exists).

palette_base_theme.description:
Currently active theme preset name, and/or the base for the
palette when the palette has been modified. Persisted backing
state for the theme selector; not a labeled setting of its own.

pywal-apply-globally.description: Apply pywal colors to terminals and external tools.

rounding.description: Corner rounding for dropdowns, popovers, and dialogs.

scale.description: Scale multiplier for dropdowns, popovers, and dialogs.

theming-monitor.description: Monitor whose wallpaper drives color extraction. Empty uses the first available.

wallust-apply-globally.description: Apply wallust colors to terminals and external tools.

wallust-colorspace.description: Wallust color space for dominant color selection.

SurfaceAnimation.description:
Per-surface enter/exit animation override. Any field left unset falls back
to the global `[animations]` enter/exit, then to `transition`/`duration`.

enter.description: Enter transition for this surface. Unset → global `enter`, then `transition`.

enter-duration.description: Enter duration in ms. Unset → global `enter-duration`, then `duration`.

exit.description: Exit transition for this surface. Unset → global `exit`, then `transition`.

exit-duration.description: Exit duration in ms. Unset → global `exit-duration`, then `duration`.

active-color.description:
Color for the active (visible on its output) workspace.

In `background` indicator mode, also used as the button background.

active-indicator.description: Visual indicator for the active workspace.

app-icon-map.description:
Application icon mapping with glob pattern support.

Maps window `app_id` or title to symbolic icon names. Supports:
- No prefix: matches `app_id` (e.g. `"*firefox*"`)
- `app:` prefix: explicit `app_id` match (e.g. `"app:org.mozilla.*"`)
- `title:` prefix: matches window title (e.g. `"title:*YouTube*"`)

## Example

```toml
[modules.sway-workspaces.app-icon-map]
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"title:*YouTube*" = "ld-youtube-symbolic"
```

app-icons-dedupe.description:
Deduplicate application icons within a workspace.

When `true`, one icon per unique `app_id`. When `false`, one icon
per window.

app-icons-empty.description: Icon shown when a workspace has no application windows.

app-icons-fallback.description: Fallback icon for applications not matched by `app-icon-map`.

app-icons-show.description: Show application icons for windows on each workspace.

border-color.description: Border color for the workspaces container.

border-show.description: Display border around the workspaces container.

container-bg-color.description: Background color for the workspaces container.

display-mode.description:
What identifies each workspace button.

- `label` (default): show the workspace label per `label-strategy`
- `icon`: show an icon from `workspace-map` (falls back to label if unmapped)
- `none`: show nothing — only app icons visible (if enabled)

divider.description: Text separator between workspace identity and app icons.

empty-color.description: Color for empty workspaces and placeholder slots.

hide-trailing-empty.description:
Hide the trailing empty workspace on each output.

Off by default, since sway does not auto-allocate a trailing empty
workspace the way niri does. Enable it if your config keeps one around.

icon-gap.description: Gap between app icons within a workspace button. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"4px"`).

icon-size.description:
Workspace icon size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

Applies to identity icons and custom icons from `workspace-map`.

label-size.description: Workspace label and divider size. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"16px"`).

label-strategy.description:
How to compose the workspace label when `display-mode = "label"`.

- `index`: number only (`"1"`, `"2"`)
- `name-or-index` (default): name when set, number otherwise
- `name-only`: name only; unnamed workspaces show nothing
- `index-and-name`: `"1: web"` form; unnamed workspaces show the number alone

monitor-specific.description:
Show only workspaces on this bar's monitor.

When `true` (default), each bar shows only its own output's
workspaces. When `false`, all workspaces from every output are shown.

occupied-color.description: Color for occupied workspaces (have windows but not active).

urgent-mode.description:
Where the urgent pulse is applied.

- `workspace` (default): whole button pulses
- `application`: only the urgent app icon pulses, falling back to
  `workspace` when app icons are disabled

urgent-show.description: Pulse animation on workspaces with urgent windows.

workspace-ignore.description:
Workspaces to hide from the display.

Glob patterns matched against the workspace's name, then its number,
then its stable id. Examples:
- `"scratch"` — hide the workspace named `scratch`
- `"1?"` — hide numbers 10-19

Per-workspace icon and color overrides, keyed by name or id-as-string.

## Example

```toml
[modules.sway-workspaces.workspace-map]
web = { icon = "ld-globe-symbolic", color = "#4a90d9" }
```

workspace-padding.description: Padding for workspace content along the bar direction. Accepts a scale multiplier or pixels (e.g. `"8px"`).

```toml
name = "*discord*"
```

Dynamic providers (Matugen, Pywal, Wallust) inject palette tokens at runtime.

oneOf.description: Static theming using Wayle's built-in palettes.

ThresholdEntry.description:
A threshold entry that maps a numeric value range to color overrides.

At least one of `above` or `below` must be set. When both are set,
both conditions must be satisfied (AND logic).

## TOML Example

```toml
[[modules.cpu.thresholds]]
above = 70
```

above.description: Activate when metric value >= this threshold.

below.description: Activate when metric value <= this threshold.

border-color.description: Override border color when threshold is active.

button-bg-color.description: Override button background color when threshold is active.

icon-bg-color.description: Override icon background color when threshold is active.

label-color.description: Override label color when threshold is active.

ToastPreset.description:
A reusable toast preset, triggerable by id with `wayle toast --preset <id>`.

A preset captures a toast's text and icon so it can be fired by name. The
label/icon can still be overridden per invocation, and runtime-only fields
(`--percentage`, `--duration`, `--class`) are supplied at invoke time, not
stored on the preset. Duration always follows the OSD config.

## Example

```toml
[[osd.presets]]
id = "saved"
label = "Saved"
icon = "ld-check-symbolic"

# Fire it: wayle toast --preset saved
# With a progress bar: wayle toast --preset saved --percentage 80
```

icon.description: Symbolic icon name shown beside the text.

label.description: Toast text. An explicit label on the command line overrides this.

name.description:

Examples: `"discord"`, `"*Discord*"`, `"org.kde.*"`

Minimum urgency level that shows a colored urgency bar on popup cards.

All urgency levels at or above the threshold display the bar.
For example, `Normal` shows bars on both normal and critical popups.

oneOf.description: Show urgency bars on all popups.
Show urgency bars on normal and critical popups.
Show urgency bars on critical popups only.

oneOf.description:
Pulse only the app icon(s) belonging to the urgent window.

Falls back to `workspace` when app icons are disabled.

empty to disable. Takes precedence over the single `wallpaper` image.

```toml
[[wallpaper.monitors]]
name = "HDMI-1"
wallpaper = "/home/me/pictures/wall-secondary.png"
fit-mode = "fit"
```

WeatherConfig.description: Current conditions with hourly and daily forecasts in a dropdown.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ temp }}` - Current temperature (e.g., "72")
- `{{ temp_unit }}` - Temperature unit symbol ("°F" or "°C")
- `{{ feels_like }}` - Feels-like temperature
- `{{ condition }}` - Weather condition text (e.g., "Cloudy")
- `{{ humidity }}` - Humidity percentage (e.g., "65%")
- `{{ wind_speed }}` - Wind speed with unit (e.g., "12 km/h")
- `{{ wind_dir }}` - Wind direction (e.g., "NW")
- `{{ high }}` - Today's high temperature
- `{{ low }}` - Today's low temperature

## Examples

- `"{{ temp }}{{ temp_unit }}"` - "22°C"
- `"{{ temp }}{{ temp_unit }} {{ condition }}"` - "22°C Partly Cloudy"
- `"{{ temp }}{{ temp_unit }} H:{{ high }} L:{{ low }}"` - "22°C H:25 L:18"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color. Auto selects based on variant for contrast.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

location.description: Location for weather data (city name or "lat,lon" coordinates).

time-format.description: Time display format for sunrise/sunset and hourly forecast.

visual-crossing-key.description:
Visual Crossing API key. Supports `$VAR_NAME` syntax to reference
environment variables from `.*.env` files in the config directory.

weatherapi-key.description:
WeatherAPI.com API key. Supports `$VAR_NAME` syntax to reference
environment variables from `.*.env` files in the config directory.

WeekStart.description: Which day appears in the first column of the calendar dropdown.

WindowTitleConfig.description: Active window title with optional app-icon prefix.

format.description:
Format string for the label.

## Placeholders

- `{{ title }}` - Window title
- `{{ app }}` - Application name (WM_CLASS on Hyprland)

## Examples

- `"{{ title }}"` - "README.md - VSCode"
- `"{{ app }}: {{ title }}"` - "firefox: GitHub"

icon-color.description: Icon foreground color.

icon-mappings.description:
Icon mappings. Glob patterns to icon names.

Keys are patterns matching the window class (default) or title (when
prefixed with `title:`). Values are icon names from the installed icon
set. User mappings are checked before built-in mappings.

## Example

```toml
[modules.window-title.icon-mappings]
"*firefox*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"org.mozilla.*" = "ld-globe-symbolic"
"title:*YouTube*" = "ld-youtube-symbolic"
```

icon-name.description: Fallback icon when no mapping matches.

label-max-length.description: Max label characters before truncation with ellipsis. Set to 0 to disable.

WorkspaceClickAction.description: Click/scroll action: focus:this | focus:next | focus:previous | focus:last | dropdown:NAME | shell command | empty for none

WorkspaceMap.description:
Per-workspace icon and color overrides, keyed by workspace ID.

TOML table keys are always strings, so `"1"` parses into the workspace
with ID `1`. Negative IDs refer to Hyprland's special workspaces. Keys
that don't appear in the map fall back to the default behaviour set by
[`HyprlandWorkspacesConfig::display_mode`].

## Examples

```toml
[modules.hyprland-workspaces.workspace-map]
# Whole entry on one line with an inline table
1 = { label = "Web", icon = "ld-globe-symbolic", color = "#4a90d9" }
2 = { icon = "ld-terminal-symbolic" }
3 = { icon = "ld-code-symbolic", color = "accent" }

# Or spread the entry across its own subtable
[modules.hyprland-workspaces.workspace-map.4]
label = "Chat"
icon = "ld-message-square-symbolic"
color = "status-success"

# Negative IDs target Hyprland special workspaces
[modules.hyprland-workspaces.workspace-map.-99]
icon = "ld-scratch-symbolic"
```

color.description: Custom background color for this workspace when active.

icon.description:
Custom icon for this workspace. When set, the icon is shown regardless
of the module's `display-mode`, so a row can mix labelled workspaces
with icon-only ones (e.g. `[1][2][icon]`).

label.description: Text shown instead of the workspace's name or index.

WorldClockConfig.description: Multiple timezones shown together in a dropdown.

format.description:
Format string with embedded timezone blocks.

Use `{{ tz('timezone', 'strftime') }}` to insert a formatted time.
Anything outside a placeholder stays as literal text.

## Examples

| Format string | Renders as |
|---|---|
| `"{{ tz('UTC', '%H:%M %Z') }}"` | `14:30 UTC` |
| `"NYC {{ tz('America/New_York', '%H:%M') }}  TYO {{ tz('Asia/Tokyo', '%H:%M') }}"` | `NYC 09:30  TYO 23:30` |
| `"{{ tz('America/New_York', '%H:%M %Z') }} \| {{ tz('Europe/London', '%H:%M %Z') }}"` | `09:30 EST \| 14:30 GMT` |

description:
Main configuration structure for Wayle.

Represents the complete configuration schema that can be loaded
from TOML files. All fields have sensible defaults.

imports.description:
TOML files to import and merge before this config.

Paths are relative to the config file.
Imported values are overridden by values in this file.

```toml
imports = ["themes.toml", "modules/clock.toml"]
```
